result.stdout:
Description:
  GitLab Converter

Usage:
  GitLabReporter [command] [options]

Options:
  -?, -h, --help  Show help and usage information
  --version       Show version information

Commands:
  convert  Convert a report from one format to another
  summary  Outputs a summary of issues found in a GitLab report and optionally exists with a non-zero return value

commands.payload:
Description:
  GitLab Converter

Usage:
  GitLabReporter [command] [options]

Options:
  -?, -h, --help  Show help and usage information
  --version       Show version information

Commands:
  convert  Convert a report from one format to another
  summary  Outputs a summary of issues found in a GitLab report and optionally exists with a non-zero return value

result.stdout:
Description:
  Convert a report from one format to another

Usage:
  GitLabReporter convert [command] [options]

Options:
  -?, -h, --help  Show help and usage information

Commands:
  dotnet-format  Convert dotnet format output to GitLab format
  yamllint       Convert yamllint (parsable) output to GitLab format

commands.payload:
Description:
  Convert a report from one format to another

Usage:
  GitLabReporter convert [command] [options]

Options:
  -?, -h, --help  Show help and usage information

Commands:
  dotnet-format  Convert dotnet format output to GitLab format
  yamllint       Convert yamllint (parsable) output to GitLab format

result.stdout:
Description:
  Convert dotnet format output to GitLab format

Usage:
  GitLabReporter convert dotnet-format [options]

Options:
  -i, --input-file (REQUIRED)   Path to the input file containing a report
  -o, --output-file (REQUIRED)  Path to the output file where the converted report will be saved
  -v, --verbose                 Enable verbose output [default: False]
  -f, --force                   Force overwrite of the output file if it already exists [default: False]
  -?, -h, --help                Show help and usage information

result.stdout:
Description:
  Outputs a summary of issues found in a GitLab report and optionally exists with a non-zero return value

Usage:
  GitLabReporter summary [options]

Options:
  --report-file (REQUIRED)                                     Path to the report file
  --fail-on-severity <blocker|critical|info|major|minor|none>  Set the minimum severity level that will cause the command to exit with a non-zero status. Options are: info, minor, major, critical, blocker. [default: none]
  -?, -h, --help                                               Show help and usage information
